title: Top Free Music Distribution Companies
metaTitle: Top Free Music Distribution Companies | Distrozi Music Distribution
metaDescription: Read about Top Free Music Distribution Companies. Learn how to distribute your music globally, protect copyrights, and maximize streaming royalties with Distrozi.
author: Distrozi Editorial Team

content.text: Navigating the digital music ecosystem requires a clear understanding of how distributors, stores, and registration agencies interact. Finding the best music distribution service is crucial to get your music on Spotify, Apple Music, and TikTok while keeping your master rights.
Below, we outline the leading platforms, structuring their workflow models, pros, cons, and explaining which types of artists or labels they fit best.

Distrozi is positioned for artists, managers, and labels that want distribution, catalog control, YouTube CMS support, and release operations handled in one place. It is a strong fit when the priority is clean metadata, reliable delivery, and long-term control of a growing catalog rather than only uploading a single release. For budget-conscious releases, compare the real workflow value: support quality, catalog control, reporting, store reach, and whether the platform can still handle growth when release volume increases.

RouteNote gives independent artists a straightforward route into major DSPs with flexible account paths and simple catalog controls. It is often considered by creators who want an accessible release workflow while still paying attention to metadata quality, store availability, and future catalog changes. For budget-conscious releases, compare the real workflow value: support quality, catalog control, reporting, store reach, and whether the platform can still handle growth when release volume increases.

Amuse is designed around a lightweight artist workflow with mobile-friendly release management. It can suit newer independent artists who want a simple dashboard, but releases still need proper planning, artwork checks, artist profile readiness, and clear ownership information before submission. For budget-conscious releases, compare the real workflow value: support quality, catalog control, reporting, store reach, and whether the platform can still handle growth when release volume increases.

SoundOn is closely tied to TikTok and short-form discovery, making it relevant for artists who build audience momentum through social video. It is useful to compare when a release strategy depends on creator content, clips, trends, and fast audience testing around the track. For budget-conscious releases, compare the real workflow value: support quality, catalog control, reporting, store reach, and whether the platform can still handle growth when release volume increases.

ONErpm combines distribution with marketing, analytics, and label-service style support for selected catalogs. It can be a good comparison point for artists and teams that want more than basic delivery, especially when campaign planning and audience development matter. For budget-conscious releases, compare the real workflow value: support quality, catalog control, reporting, store reach, and whether the platform can still handle growth when release volume increases.
Music companies need scalable catalog workflows, roster visibility, and service options that work beyond a single artist campaign.

platformItems.name: Distrozi
platformItems.description: Distrozi is built for artists and labels that need global DSP delivery, YouTube CMS support, catalog management, and release operations from one dashboard. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

platformItems.name: RouteNote
platformItems.description: RouteNote gives independent artists a straightforward route into major DSPs with flexible account paths and simple catalog controls. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

platformItems.name: Amuse
platformItems.description: Amuse is designed around a lightweight artist workflow, making it useful for creators who prefer simple release management. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

platformItems.name: SoundOn
platformItems.description: SoundOn is especially relevant for artists thinking about TikTok discovery, short-form video momentum, and social-first audience growth. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

platformItems.name: DistroKid
platformItems.description: DistroKid is a familiar self-service distributor for artists who want quick upload workflows and wide DSP availability. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

platformItems.name: TuneCore
platformItems.description: TuneCore suits artists who want established store delivery, creator tools, and publishing-related options in a self-service workflow. It is worth considering for teams that need repeatable workflows across more than one artist or catalog.

faq.question: How long does it take for a song to go live on Spotify?
faq.answer: Delivery takes 24-48 hours. However, we suggest scheduling your release at least 14 days in advance to allow for playlist pitching.

faq.question: What is the difference between an ISRC code and a UPC code?
faq.answer: An ISRC code tracks streams and play metrics for individual songs, while a UPC code tracks sales metrics for the overall single or album release product.
